title: salix arctica subsp. torulosa
intro: Salix arctica subsp. torulosa, also known as the Arctic willow, is a subspecies of the Arctic willow tree, native to the Arctic regions of North America and Greenland. The medicinal properties of Salix arctica subsp. torulosa include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which are attributed to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of the plant include reducing inflammation and relieving pain, as well as providing protection against oxidative stress and cell damage. The herbal preparations of Salix arctica subsp. torulosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to alleviate symptoms of arthritis, reduce fever, and promote overall well-being.
